message:
test(e2e): add multi-process swarm-writer harness (#79)

Adds crates/fsqlite-e2e/tests/swarm_writer_harness.rs implementing the
short-lived multi-process workload from #79:

  N = 16 (configurable via FSQLITE_SWARM_WORKERS)
  duration = 30s (configurable via FSQLITE_SWARM_SECONDS)
  process lifetime = uniform [50ms, 500ms]
  ops per process = uniform [1, 8] from {INSERT, UPDATE, SELECT_BY_PK,
                                          SELECT_RANGE}
  key space = 0..1000 (configurable; small enough that overlap is the
                       common case)

Self-respawn via std::env::current_exe() + the FSQLITE_SWARM_CHILD env
marker (works on Unix and Windows, unlike nix::unistd::fork). Each
child writes a per-process JSONL audit log; the parent collects and
analyzes after the run.

Acceptance assertions are graded against issue #79's enumerated list:
integrity_check on stock SQLite, WAL shape, no silent zero-row returns,
no wrong-row returns, sequential-open consistency, busy_timeout
honored, plus a VACUUM INTO + integrity_check escape hatch as #79
explicitly requested.

The harness can be run against fsqlite, stock sqlite3 (rusqlite), or
both for differential checking — anything fsqlite gets wrong that
stock gets right is a fsqlite bug.

Marked #[ignore]; runs on demand via:

  cargo test --release -p fsqlite-e2e --test swarm_writer_harness \\
      -- --ignored --nocapture --test-threads=1

Today this harness FAILS by design — it exists to expose the
corrupt-under-multi-process bug family cataloged in #70 (beads_rust
#252/#254/#255 plus frankensqlite #56/#65/#69). Each known-failing
assertion carries an inline reference to the bug shape it covers; do
NOT silence failures, file engineering work under #70.

Companion to docs(concurrency) #80.

Closes #79

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
